title: Amy Carlson

infobox.value: Amy Lynn Carlson   (1968-07-07) July 7, 1968 (age 57)[[1]](#cite_note-dailyxp-1)  [Elmhurst, Illinois](/wiki/Elmhurst,_Illinois "Elmhurst, Illinois"), U.S.[[1]](#cite_note-dailyxp-1)

(born July 7, 1968) is an American actress known for her roles as Linda Reagan in the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") police procedural *[Blue Bloods](/wiki/Blue_Bloods_(TV_series) "Blue Bloods (TV series)"),* Alex Taylor on the NBC drama *[Third Watch](/wiki/Third_Watch "Third Watch")*, and Josie Watts in the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") daytime soap opera [*Another World*](/wiki/Another_World_(TV_series) "Another World (TV series)").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actress (born 1968)

For the religious leader, see [Amy Carlson (religious leader)](/wiki/Amy_Carlson_(religious_leader) "Amy Carlson (religious leader)").

| Amy Carlson | |
| --- | --- |
| Carlson in 2015 | |
| Born | Amy Lynn Carlson   (1968-07-07) July 7, 1968 (age 57)[[1]](#cite_note-dailyxp-1)  [Elmhurst, Illinois](/wiki/Elmhurst,_Illinois "Elmhurst, Illinois"), U.S.[[1]](#cite_note-dailyxp-1) |
| Alma mater | [Knox College](/wiki/Knox_College_(Illinois) "Knox College (Illinois)") |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 1992–present |
| Spouse | [Syd Butler](/wiki/Syd_Butler "Syd Butler")  ​ (m. 2004)​ |
| Children | 2 |

**Amy Lynn Carlson** (born July 7, 1968) is an American actress known for her roles as Linda Reagan in the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") police procedural *[Blue Bloods](/wiki/Blue_Bloods_(TV_series) "Blue Bloods (TV series)"),* Alex Taylor on the NBC drama *[Third Watch](/wiki/Third_Watch "Third Watch")*, and Josie Watts in the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") daytime soap opera [*Another World*](/wiki/Another_World_(TV_series) "Another World (TV series)").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Carlson was born in [Elmhurst](/wiki/Elmhurst,_Illinois "Elmhurst, Illinois"), [DuPage County, Illinois](/wiki/DuPage_County,_Illinois "DuPage County, Illinois"), near [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), and was raised in [Glen Ellyn, Illinois](/wiki/Glen_Ellyn,_Illinois "Glen Ellyn, Illinois"),[[2]](#cite_note-tvg-2) the daughter of schoolteacher parents, Barbara Jane (Hultman) and Robert Eugene Carlson. She is of [Swedish](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") descent, with roots in [Småland](/wiki/Sm%C3%A5land "Småland").[[3]](#cite_note-pbs-episode-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### 1986–1992: College and early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1986–1992: College and early career")]

Her first acting job was as a background actor in the film [*Lucas*](/wiki/Lucas_(film) "Lucas (film)") starring [Charlie Sheen](/wiki/Charlie_Sheen "Charlie Sheen"), [Corey Haim](/wiki/Corey_Haim "Corey Haim"), and [Winona Ryder](/wiki/Winona_Ryder "Winona Ryder"). Later she followed her older sister Betsy to [Knox College](/wiki/Knox_College_(Illinois) "Knox College (Illinois)") in [Galesburg, Illinois](/wiki/Galesburg,_Illinois "Galesburg, Illinois").[[2]](#cite_note-tvg-2) While in college, Carlson was active in the school's theatre department, appearing in *[Fifth of July](/wiki/Fifth_of_July_(play) "Fifth of July (play)")*, *[Noises Off](/wiki/Noises_Off "Noises Off")*, *[A Lie of the Mind](/wiki/A_Lie_of_the_Mind "A Lie of the Mind")*, and *[The School for Scandal](/wiki/The_School_for_Scandal "The School for Scandal")*. She directed the play *[Aunt Dan and Lemon](/wiki/Aunt_Dan_and_Lemon "Aunt Dan and Lemon")* written by [Wallace Shawn](/wiki/Wallace_Shawn "Wallace Shawn") on her senior year. Carlson graduated [cum laude](/wiki/Cum_laude "Cum laude") with a degree in East Asian Studies with a concentration in theater.[[4]](#cite_note-amycarlson.com-4)

After graduating college, Carlson moved to Chicago where she studied improv with [Charna Halpern](/wiki/Charna_Halpern "Charna Halpern") at the Harold [ImprovOlympic](/wiki/IO_Theater "IO Theater"), and took acting classes at The Actor's Center with Victor D'Altorio and Eileen Vorbach. She also appeared in some small theater productions including *Dark City, Revenge of the Cheerleader* with [Warren Leight](/wiki/Warren_Leight "Warren Leight") and Theater of the Film Noir at the Folio Theater Company. She also appeared in three episodes of *[The Untouchables](/wiki/The_Untouchables_(1993_TV_series) "The Untouchables (1993 TV series)")* starring [William Forsythe](/wiki/William_Forsythe_(actor) "William Forsythe (actor)") and [Tom Amandes](/wiki/Tom_Amandes "Tom Amandes"). Carlson also appeared in three episodes of *[Missing Persons](/wiki/Missing_Persons_(TV_series) "Missing Persons (TV series)")* with [Daniel J. Travanti](/wiki/Daniel_J._Travanti "Daniel J. Travanti"). She also played in *Legacy of Lies* a TV movie with [Joe Morton](/wiki/Joe_Morton "Joe Morton").

### 1993–2003: Television contracts, films and relief work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 1993–2003: Television contracts, films and relief work")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f0/Amy_Carlson%2C_May_2002_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Amy_Carlson%2C_May_2002_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Amy_Carlson,_May_2002_(cropped).jpg)

Carlson in 2002

Carlson landed the role of Josie Watts and moved to New York in late December 1993 to make her soap opera debut on *[Another World](/wiki/Another_World_(TV_series) "Another World (TV series)").* During her first year, she traveled with [World Vision](/wiki/World_Vision_International "World Vision International") to [Rwanda](/wiki/Rwanda "Rwanda") to work on an awareness campaign after the war.

Later, she was nominated for a [Daytime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actress in a Drama Series](/wiki/Daytime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Supporting_Actress_in_a_Drama_Series "Daytime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actress in a Drama Series") in 1998[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]. After leaving *Another World* she appeared in *[Thanks of a Grateful Nation](/wiki/Thanks_of_a_Grateful_Nation "Thanks of a Grateful Nation")*, about Persian Gulf War syndrome.

Carlson moved to Los Angeles in 1998, where she guest-starred in a number of prime time shows including *[NYPD Blue](/wiki/NYPD_Blue "NYPD Blue"),* had a recurring role on the show [*Get Real*](/wiki/Get_Real_(American_TV_series) "Get Real (American TV series)") which starred [Anne Hathaway](/wiki/Anne_Hathaway "Anne Hathaway") and [Jesse Eisenberg](/wiki/Jesse_Eisenberg "Jesse Eisenberg"), and filmed [*If These Walls Could Talk Part 2*](/wiki/If_These_Walls_Could_Talk_2 "If These Walls Could Talk 2") directed by [Martha Coolidge](/wiki/Martha_Coolidge "Martha Coolidge"). While in LA, she tested against [Mariska Hargitay](/wiki/Mariska_Hargitay "Mariska Hargitay") and [Angie Harmon](/wiki/Angie_Harmon "Angie Harmon") for the role that Mariska won in *[Law & Order: Special Victims Unit](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Special_Victims_Unit "Law & Order: Special Victims Unit").* Soon after, she started acting in the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") TV Series [*Falcone*](/wiki/Falcone_(TV_series) "Falcone (TV series)") as [Donny Brasco's](/wiki/Joseph_D._Pistone "Joseph D. Pistone") wife, shot in Toronto. She later started guest starring in *Law & Order: SVU* and filming independent films such as *[Winning Girls Through Psychic Mind Control](/wiki/Winning_Girls_Through_Psychic_Mind_Control "Winning Girls Through Psychic Mind Control")* directed by [Barry Alexander Brown](/wiki/Barry_Alexander_Brown "Barry Alexander Brown") where she played a singer, with the voice of [Regina Spektor](/wiki/Regina_Spektor "Regina Spektor").

Between 2000 and 2003 she starred as Alex Taylor in *[Third Watch](/wiki/Third_Watch "Third Watch").*[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### 2004–2017: Prime time highlights and children

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2004–2017: Prime time highlights and children")]

After leaving *Third Watch*, Carlson went on to star on [*Peacemakers*](/wiki/Peacemakers_(TV_series) "Peacemakers (TV series)"), a *CSI*-inspired show set in the late 1800s, starring opposite [Tom Berenger](/wiki/Tom_Berenger "Tom Berenger") and [Peter O'Meara](/wiki/Peter_O%27Meara "Peter O'Meara"). When the show was not renewed, she worked on several TV shows and films including a *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* episode on its 15th season entitled ["Dead Wives Club"](/wiki/Law_%26_Order_season_15#ep327 "Law & Order season 15"). Soon [Dick Wolf](/wiki/Dick_Wolf "Dick Wolf") was calling to sign her to *[Law & Order: Trial by Jury](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Trial_by_Jury "Law & Order: Trial by Jury")* where she co-starred alongside [Bebe Neuwirth](/wiki/Bebe_Neuwirth "Bebe Neuwirth"), [Jerry Orbach](/wiki/Jerry_Orbach "Jerry Orbach"), [Kirk Acevedo](/wiki/Kirk_Acevedo "Kirk Acevedo"), [Fred Thompson](/wiki/Fred_Thompson "Fred Thompson"), and [Scott Cohen](/wiki/Scott_Cohen_(actor) "Scott Cohen (actor)"). Carlson continued to work on a variety of film and television roles such as Guest Starring roles on *[Criminal Minds](/wiki/Criminal_Minds "Criminal Minds")* and [*Fringe*](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)"). On 2010, she landed the role of [Linda Reagan](/wiki/Linda_Reagan "Linda Reagan") on [*Blue Bloods*](/wiki/Blue_Bloods_(TV_series) "Blue Bloods (TV series)"). Carlson continued in the role of Linda through the seventh season of *Blue Bloods*. During her hiatus, she played Erin Callan, working alongside [James Woods](/wiki/James_Woods "James Woods") in [*Too Big to Fail*](/wiki/Too_Big_to_Fail_(film) "Too Big to Fail (film)"), directed by [Curtis Hanson](/wiki/Curtis_Hanson "Curtis Hanson") from the book written by [Andrew Ross Sorkin](/wiki/Andrew_Ross_Sorkin "Andrew Ross Sorkin"). She was also written for the role of Christina Cassertes, by her friend [David Cross](/wiki/David_Cross "David Cross") in his directorial debut film, [*Hits*](/wiki/Hits_(film) "Hits (film)"). After seven years on *Blue Bloods*, Carlson's contract came to an end. In the eighth-season premiere episode, which aired on September 29, 2017, it was revealed that Linda—who was a nurse—had died in a helicopter crash while transporting a patient.

### 2018–present: Current work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2018–present: Current work")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/Amy_Carlson_at_PaleyFest_2014.jpg/250px-Amy_Carlson_at_PaleyFest_2014.jpg)](/wiki/File:Amy_Carlson_at_PaleyFest_2014.jpg)

Carlson in 2014

Following *Blue Bloods*, Carlson worked recurring roles on [*The Society*](/wiki/The_Society_(TV_series) "The Society (TV series)") and *[The Village](/wiki/The_Village_(2019_TV_series) "The Village (2019 TV series)")*. She also shot films *Sunny Daze, The Incoherents, [A Bread Factory](/wiki/A_Bread_Factory "A Bread Factory") Part One,* and the indie horror film *Know Fear*.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Just prior to COVID-19 shutdowns, Carlson co-wrote, directed and starred in a short film, *The Letter*, co-written by [Syd Butler](/wiki/Syd_Butler "Syd Butler"). She cast her friend from *Law & Order: Trial by Jury*, [Scott Cohen](/wiki/Scott_Cohen_(actor) "Scott Cohen (actor)"), as her co-star. Her work was honored with best director and actor at the Hollywood International Women's Film Festival,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] as well as awards with the [Cannes Indie Film Festival](/wiki/2020_Cannes_Film_Festival "2020 Cannes Film Festival"), Hudson Valley Film Festival and Dark Women Film Festival.

During the 2020 pandemic, Carlson and husband Syd Butler along with his bandmate Seth Jabour ([Les Savy Fav](/wiki/Les_Savy_Fav "Les Savy Fav"), [The 8G Band](/wiki/The_8G_Band "The 8G Band")), who together form the band Office Romance, finished and released their second album and first full-length album, *Holidays of Love*.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 2021, Carlson began shooting as a recurring cast member on *[FBI: Most Wanted](/wiki/FBI:_Most_Wanted "FBI: Most Wanted")*, opposite her friend and co-star from *Another World*, [Julian McMahon](/wiki/Julian_McMahon "Julian McMahon").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Carlson resides in New York City with her husband [Syd Butler](/wiki/Syd_Butler "Syd Butler").[[2]](#cite_note-tvg-2)[[1]](#cite_note-dailyxp-1) They have two children.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[1]](#cite_note-dailyxp-1)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Amy_Carlson&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Awards")]

In 2018, Carlson was presented with the Muhammad Ali Award for Gender Equality.[[11]](#cite_note-11) In 2021, she won a Knox College Alumni Achievement Award.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
